lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: You move quietly. I almost didn't hear you.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: That was the idea. I was watching the grain store.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: I already cleared it. Three rats since dawn.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: I know. I saw. You fight like you've got something to prove.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Maybe I do. This guild doesn't build itself.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: ...No. But you don't have to build it alone.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Do you ever rest? You've been at the board since first light.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Resting is how you lose what you've built.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Resting is how you keep it. Listen to the rain for a moment.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: ...Fine. One moment.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: The sound of water on stone. That's what home sounds like.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Home. I haven't thought of anywhere that way in a long time.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: When the plague rats came, you didn't run.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Neither did you. Though you had more reason to.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: You built this from nothing. You had the most to lose.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: I had the most to fight for. That's different.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Then I'll fight beside you. For as long as the guild stands.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: I've mapped the courier routes. Three optimal paths to the monastery.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: The monks' cat told me something is wrong in the scriptorium. I can feel it.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Feel it. You can feel it.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Mock if you like. My whiskers know things your maps don't.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: I'm not mocking. I'm... cataloguing.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: That noble's letter you delivered. It smelled like ash and lavender.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: It was sealed. You shouldn't have been sniffing it.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Ash means grief. Lavender means someone wants to forget.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Or someone was burning herbs near their writing desk.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Possibly. But my way is more interesting, isn't it?

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: ...Annoyingly, yes.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: I've been thinking about what you said. About the omens.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: A formal cat like you, thinking about omens?

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Your intuitions have been right more often than chance allows. That's... data.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: And your routes have kept us safe every time. That's a kind of wisdom too.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Perhaps the guild needs both. The map and the whisker.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Now you're talking sense.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: You're fast. Faster than any cat I've seen.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Speed keeps you alive when you're small.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: I wouldn't know about small. But I can hold a door all night.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: That's... actually useful. The granary gate doesn't latch properly.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Show me. I'll keep it shut.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Why did you come here? You could guard any barn in the county.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Barns don't need protecting. They just need sitting in.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: And a guild does?

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: A guild has cats who care about each other. That's worth more than grain.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: You're more thoughtful than you look.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Big body, big heart. It's a package deal.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: The plague rats almost took the guildhall. I held the door, but barely.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: I saw the scratches. You should have called for help.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: You were clearing the cathedral. The town needed you more.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: The guild needs you too. Don't forget that.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: I won't. As long as you remember you don't have to carry everything alone.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: ...Deal.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: You were at the warehouse before dawn. I saw your paw prints.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: I prefer working before the town wakes. Fewer distractions.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Efficient. I respect that.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: And you always deliver letters on time. The monks have noticed.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Punctuality is the simplest form of respect.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: I've been tracking our earnings. You consistently outperform on pest control.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: I don't do it for the numbers.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: I know. That's what makes the numbers so interesting.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: You see patterns in everything, don't you?

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Someone has to. Otherwise it's just cats chasing mice.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Sometimes chasing mice is enough.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: You never ask where I go at night.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: You always come back. That's sufficient.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Most cats would be suspicious.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Suspicion is a waste of energy spent on cats who've already proven themselves.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: ...That might be the kindest thing anyone's said to me.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Don't tell the Siamese. I have a reputation.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: You're so quiet I sometimes forget you're in the room.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: And you're so loud I never forget.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Loud! I'm expressive. There's a difference.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: If you say so.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: I do say so. Frequently. And at volume.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: You were on the roof again last night. I could hear you singing.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: It wasn't singing. It was... communing.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: With the moon?

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: With whatever listens. Don't you ever feel something watching back?

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: ...Sometimes. In the grain store, when it's very still.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: See? You understand more than you let on.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: I had a vision about you. Don't laugh.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: I'm not laughing.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: You were standing in a great hall. Sunlight through stained glass. Cats everywhere.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: That sounds like the guildhall. On a good day.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: It was the guildhall. Years from now. And you were still here.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Then I suppose I'd better stay.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Sorry about the door frame. I keep misjudging my shoulders.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: You took out two boards. I measured.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: I'll fix them. I'm good with wood.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: You're good with everything that doesn't require fitting through normal-sized openings.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Fair point.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: The dock foreman asked for you specifically. Said you're the only cat who intimidates the ship rats.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: I don't try to intimidate anyone.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: That's exactly why it works. Genuine presence, not performance.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: You'd know about performance. You walk like you're always being watched.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Because I usually am. Observation is a two-way street.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: I just walk. People get out of the way on their own.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: When the plague hit the docks, you had the evacuation planned in minutes.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: And you carried three kittens out while the water rose. Plans don't do that.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Someone has to carry things. It's what I'm for.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: You're not 'for' anything. You choose to help. That matters more.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: ...Thank you. That's not something big cats hear often.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Then people aren't paying attention.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Hold still. I'm reading your aura.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: My what now?

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Your energy. It's... remarkably calm. Like a warm hearth.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: I've been told I run warm. Thick fur.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: It's more than fur. You make other cats feel safe just by being nearby.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: That story you told the kittens about Saint Gertrude. Was any of it true?

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: All of it was true. Some of it happened.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: That doesn't make sense.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Truth and facts are cousins, not twins. The kittens understood.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: They also believe I'm part bear.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Are you sure you're not?

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: I dreamed the guild burned. I woke up terrified.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: It won't burn. I check the hearth every night.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Every night since I came here. Old habit from the barns.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Then my dream was wrong. That's the first time.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Maybe it wasn't wrong. Maybe it was a warning, and I already answered it.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: You reorganized the job board.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: By difficulty, then by reward ratio. It was chaos before.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: It was my chaos. I knew where everything was.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Did you though?

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: ...Fine. It's better now.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: The other cats look to you. You know that.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: They shouldn't. I'm just the first one who showed up.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: That's exactly why they look to you. First through the door, last to leave.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Someone has to be.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: And someone has to make sure the leader doesn't burn out. That's my job.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: I didn't assign you that.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: When I started this guild, I thought strength was all you needed.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: And now?

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Now I think you need someone who can count the fish while someone else catches them.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: A crude metaphor. But accurate.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Stay. The guild needs your kind of strength too.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: I'm not going anywhere. Someone has to keep the ledger.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: The saints sent you here. I'm sure of it.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: A storm sent me here. And an empty stomach.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Same thing, from the right angle.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: That's not how angles work.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: It is from mine.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: You talk to the chapel window. I've seen you.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: I talk to Saint Gertrude. Whether she answers is between us.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Does she?

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: She told me the plague would end. And it did.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: We ended it. With claws and sweat.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Maybe the claws were her answer.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Do you remember the night you arrived? The lightning?

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: I remember being cold and hungry and alone.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: I saw the lightning from the bell tower. One bolt, straight down into the market square.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: That's where I found you the next morning. Exactly where the bolt struck.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: ...Coincidence.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Of course. But a beautiful one.
